89-055 studying-srimad-bhagavatam-mumbai1

Event Name/Title

Studying Srimad-Bhagavatam

Event Date

1989

Event Location

Mumbai

Event Type

Temple - SB class

Transcriber

Jay Nrsimha das

Completed

13 Jul 2021

Source Audio

 89-055-Studying Bhagavatam (Mumbai)-SB_7_10_43_44-dt_89_09_28.mp3

Source Video

e.g.

Verse

SB 7.10.43-44

Tags – Places

Naimisaranaya, spiritual world, material world

Tags – Pastimes

Srimad Bhagavatam-amalpurana-how to study and become compassionate

Tags – Persons

Sukadeva gosvami, maharaja Paraksit, mahajans, Bhakti Siddhanta Saraswati Gosvami, Srila Prabhupada

Tags – Themes

Humility, faith, and respect to guru, to Srimad- Bhagavatam and the Lord

Lecture Transcription

prahrādasyānucaritaṁ
mahā-bhāgavatasya ca
bhaktir jṣānaṁ viraktiś ca
yāthārthyaṁ cāsya vai hareḥ

sarga-sthity-apyayeśasya
guṇa-karmānuvarṇanam
parāvareṣāṁ sthānānāṁ
kālena vyatyayo mahān

[SB 7.10.43-44]

[This narration describes the characteristics of the great and exalted devotee Prahlāda mahārāja, his staunch devotional service, his perfect knowledge, and his perfect detachment from material contamination. It also describes the Supreme Personality of Godhead as the cause of creation, maintenance and annihilation. Prahlāda mahārāja, in his prayers, has described the transcendental qualities of the Lord and has also described how the various abodes of the demigods and demons, regardless of how materially opulent, are destroyed by the mere direction of the Lord.]

Purport

Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam is filled with descriptions of the characteristics of various devotees, with reference to the service of the Lord. This Vedic literature is called Bhāgavatam because it deals with the Supreme Personality of Godhead and his devotee. By studying Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam under the direction of a bona fide spiritual master, one can perfectly understand the science of Kṛiṣhṇa, the nature of the material and spiritual worlds, and the aim of life. Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam  amalaṁ purāṇam. Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam is the spotless Vedic literature, as we have discussed in the beginning of Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam. Therefore, simply by understanding Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam, one can understand the science of the activities of the devotees, the activities of the demons, the permanent abode and the temporary abode. Through Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam, everything is perfectly known.

kṛṣṇe sva-dhāmopagate
dharma-jṣānādibhiḥ saha

kalau naṣṭa-dṛśām eṣa
purāṇ ārko ’dhunoditaḥ

[SB 1.3.43]

[This Bhāgavata Purāna is as brilliant as the sun, and it has arisen just after the departure of Lord Krsna to His own abode, accompanied by religion, knowledge, etc. Persons who have lost their vision due to the dense darkness of ignorance in the age of Kali shall get light from this Purāna.]

Why Krishna descends to this material world?

It was asked by the sages of Naimisaranya that, now that Lord Sri Krishna has left this world to return to his Supreme Abode, where is hope and direction to be found? In Bhagavad Gita fourth chapter, Lord Sri Krishna instructs us;

yadā yadā hi dharmasya
glānir bhavati bhārata
abhyutthānam adharmasya
tadātmānaṁ sṛjāmy aham

paritrāṇāya sādhūnāṁ
vināśāya ca duṣkṛtām
dharma-saṁsthāpan ārthāya
sambhavāmi yuge yuge

[Bg 4.7-8]

[Whenever and wherever there is a decline in religious practice, O descendant of Bharata, and a predominant rise of irreligion—at that time I descend Myself.]

[To deliver the pious and to annihilate the miscreants, as well as to reestablish the principles of religion, I Myself appear, millennium after millennium.]

‘That in order to protect the pious, annihilate the miscreants and re-establish the principles of religion, I descend on this earth. ‘Whenever there is a rise in irreligion and a decline in religion at that time the Lord descends. So Lord Krishna came to fulfill the mission of his incarnation. He came in order to give light to this world of darkness. This material world is by nature a place of darkness. If there is no light coming from an outside force, such as the sun or some sort of electrical device then by nature everything is dark. In the spiritual world, because it is the world of sat-cit-ananda, it is a world where everything consists of knowledge. Knowledge is light, therefore everything, everyone emanates its own light.

Spiritual world and material world compared. Wherever there is Krishna, there is no darkness

It is described in Bhagavad-Gita that the supreme abode of mine,

na tad bhāsayate sūryo
na śaśāṅko na pāvakaḥ
yad gatvā na nivartante
tad dhāma paramaṁ mama

[Bg 15.6]

[That supreme abode of Mine is not illumined by the sun or moon, nor by fire or electricity. Those who reach it never return to this material world.]

‘In that supreme abode of mine there is no need for sun or moon or electricity because everything is constituted by knowledge.’ Everything emanates its own pure light. The bodies of the living beings in the spiritual world, they have an effulgence, which lights up all directions and factually, because everything existing is a living body. Everything is emanating its own effulgence, every blade of grass emanates effulgence, every tree, every insect, every cow, every calf, the cowherd boys, the gopis, literally everything that exists is ‘chintamani’. ‘Cintāmaṇi-prakara-sadmasu kalpa-vṛkṣa’[Bs 5.29].It is described by Lord Brahma that abode of Lord Sri Krishna is ‘chintamani dham.’ It is a place where everything that exists is pure spiritual energy. But this material world, because it is a place of ignorance, everything is by nature dark. There is nothing that gives off its own effulgence. Therefore, without the external help of some electrical device or the sun or the moon, everything is completely in darkness due to ignorance. But when the Supreme Lord comes to this material world, which is a place of darkness, he comes to give light. ‘Light is knowledge.’

So therefore, when the Lord appeared, he especially spoke the Bhagavad-Gita to give light, to give direction to all living beings, to those who were helplessly being mistreated by the demoniac class of men such as Jarasandha, Kansa, Sishupal, Banasura, Vyomasura, Narakasura. The Supreme Lord came to protect his devotees by annihilating these miscreants. Otherwise the demoniac forces were so powerful that no innocent creature had a chance.

We read in the Srimad-Bhagavatam how Bhumi, mother earth, she could see the plight of all innocent creatures who were living within her body. She could see that the demoniac kings became so powerful that they were controlling the whole planet. And without the divine descent of the Supreme Personality of Godhead there was no hope for anyone. Therefore, Bhumi approached Lord Brahma, and Lord Brahma with the other demigods, they came to that great place, on the shore of the ocean of milk and at that place they chanted, with great devotion the Purusha-Sukta prayers and called upon Lord Hari to appear in this world. And it was at that time that the Supreme Lord, Shri Krishna, came like the sun to give light in the darkness of this material world.

Sage’s concern for the living entities after Lord Krishna’s disappearance: Who to turn for hope?

Through his transcendental pastimes, his lila, all pious and all sincere souls were given the opportunity for the ultimate benediction, of pure devotional service. So the sages of Naimisaranya, they are speaking with srila Suta Gosvami, just after the departure of Shri Krishna. And they are feeling great anxiety. In separation from the Lord, they are almost delirious, not knowing without our friend and guide Shri Krishna, here to take us by the hand and protect us and lead us forward, what hope is there? You see, when Krishna was present in this world, the age of kali could not come into being. Kali-yuga was scheduled to appear, but because of Lord Shri Krishna's divine presence, Kali- yuga could not enter into this universe. But as soon as Shri Krishna ascended again, to his eternal abode, the personification of Kali appeared on the scene of this world. So, the sages of Naimisaranya, in very, very great concern for all living creatures, they are inquiring from Suta Gosvami, ‘now that Krishna is gone, what is our hope? Where will we find light and direction in our life?’

Suta Gosvami : Srimad-Bhagavatam, an incarnation of Krishna in Kali-yuga, will give hope to all

And Suta Gosvami replied:

kṛṣṇesva-dhāmopagate
dharma-jṣān ādibhiḥ saha
kalau naṣṭa-dṛśām eṣa
purāṇ ārko ’dhunoditaḥ [SB 1.3.43]

[This Bhāgavata Purāna is as brilliant as the sun, and it has arisen just after the departure of Lord Krsna to His own abode, accompanied by religion, knowledge, etc. Persons who have lost their vision due to the dense darkness of ignorance in the age of Kali shall get light from this Purāna.]

These are factually the words of Srila Vedavyasa, the author of the Vedas. Therefore this shloka is perfectly authorized, to give the real substance of truth to all sincere humanity. Vedavyasa describes, that now that Lord Sri Krishna has left this world, he has descended through the pure absolute transcendental sound vibration of the Srimad-Bhagavatam. This Bhagavatam is the incarnation in the age of kali. The Lord has descended in this literary incarnation and this Bhagavata Purana, which is the spotless, purest of all spiritual literature's, has descended from the spiritual world specifically to give light to all sincere souls in this age of Kali-yuga. Therefore, of all the spiritual literature's, of all the great religions of the world, the most essential and important is the Srimad-Bhagavatam.

Description of Srimad-Bhagavatam and how to approach this great, transcendental science

Srimad-Bhagavatam is the graduate study of Bhagavad-Gita, which describes to us the fundamental principles of self-realization. Srimad-Bhagavatam begins where Gita leaves off. Bhagavatam begins with pure devotional service and describes how in relationship to the lord, through devotional service, we can enter into the divine lila of the Lord and exchange with him love in the mood of the five primary rasas of bhakti.

Srimad-Bhagavatam systematically brings one from the elementary level of spiritual life and with a careful study takes one to the point of the highest realizations of the supreme personification of Godhead. Therefore it is described:

naṣṭa-prāyeṣv abhadreṣu
nityaṁ bhāgavata-sevayā

bhagavaty uttama-śloke
bhaktir bhavati naiṣṭhikī

[SB 1.2.18]

[By regular attendance in the Bhagwat-class or rendering service unto the pure devotees all that is inauspicious in the heart of a candidate becomes destroyed almost to nil and thus loving service unto the Personality of Godhead, who is praised with transcendental songs, comes into being as an irrevocable fact.]

Srimad-bhagavatam must be regularly studied daily, under the direction of the person bhagavatam. The self-realized soul, who without any motivation, is the very living embodiment of the teachings of Bhagavatam. Going to hear bhagavad philosophy from a professional reciter, who is conducting the bhagavad saptah, has very little value. Because, the pure message of Bhagavatam can only be transmitted through the heart and the mouths of those persons who are surrendereda to Godhead. Bhagavat is not a business. Bhagavat is the mission of the Lord. One must hear Bhagavatam from one who was following in the footsteps of Sukadeva Gosvami; from one who has renounced all pride and prestige in this world; from one who has renounced all material attachments for sense gratification; for one who is surrendering with love to the Supreme Lord. Bhagavatam’s potency is manifested in this world through the lives of such great souls.

‘dharmaḥ projjhita-kaitavo 'tra paramo nirmatsarāṇāṁ satāṁ’ [SB 1.1.2]

The glory of Bhagavata Purana is, it kicks out all kaitava dharma. All sub-religious principles, which permeate all the great religious scriptures of the world. And it deals exclusively with prema- bhakti- pure devotion to Lord Hari.

Bhagavatam- full of exemplary pastimes of the Lord’s exalted devotees and the transcendental abode

And of course Bhagvatam is full of the most beautiful narrations of the Lord's most exalted devotees. Such great souls as the Pandavas, srila Narada muni. We read about the Pracetas and their great tapasya. We read about the great devotion of Lord Shankar. We read about Dhruva maharaja, maharaja Priyavrata, maharaja Rishabhdev . We read about Prithu maharaja, Prahlada maharaja, Ambarisa maharaja. They are the narrations of the most celebrated exalted devotees. And we read in these divine pages of how they attain perfection and how they served the Lord with a heart full of sacrifice and surrender; how they freed themselves from all material motivations and with utter humility, accepted all inconveniences as the grace of God, and never let go of his lotus feet under all circumstances. And then the Bhagavatam culminates in the tenth canto, where the divine abode of Goloka Vrindavan, which had descended into this world, is described, and the intimate lila of Shri Krishna and the brijvasis. The intimate lila of Shri Krishna and the residents of Dwaraka is described.

How to approach Sirmad-Bhagavatam? Srila Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati Thakur explains lucidly

So from the Bhagavatam, which is like a picture into the spiritual world, we can understand step by step, from the lowest state to the highest state. Therefore, Bhagavatam must be studied under the direction of a self-realized soul, very systematically. Many people are very anxious to jump into the most intimate subject matter of Bhagavatam. They are not concerned with making a very deep study of this great literature. It is described that, srila Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati Thakur, at one time he was giving Srimad-Bhagavat discourse, in Vrindavan area, and many, many people were very anxious to come, to hear the story of Lord Sri Krishna's rasa-lila of the gopis and other confidential subject matters such as this. Srila Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati Thakur, he spoke every day, twice a day, for one month. And that whole month he spoke exclusively on the first verse of Srimad-Bhagavatam. And it is described that those who were not serious, truly serious, about hearing from a pure devotee in attaining self-realization, they could not tolerate his discourses. They thought ‘what kind of a nonsense; we want to hear rasa-lila’. We want to hear of Krishna's beautiful divine pastimes in Vrajadham. What is all this philosophy- you are not this body, give up sense gratification, Krishna's the Absolute Truth. How he is the Absolute Truth? How he is great? We are advanced souls, we want to hear the.., we want to hear the nectar.’ Srila Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati, personally and specifically, stuck to the first verse in the basic philosophy; in great detail he described the basic philosophy just so these persons who take Krishna cheaply, would leave. And so that those people who were really serious about humbling themselves to understand the real science of devotion, they would remain and understand the true essence of how to live a spiritual life.

Without humility, faith and respect one cannot understand the greatness and depth of this science of God

The tendency is to take Krishna very cheaply. That is why in fact, the more basic preliminary scriptures for the yavanas and malechas, do not really describe any of the confidential lila of the Lord. Lord Jesus Christ said “I have much more to teach you, but I, but you cannot bear it now”. Certainly Lord Jesus Christ understood the divine lila of Shri Krishna. He was a pure devotee. But if he described his divine lila to these people who were still addicted to sinful activities, of all varieties; the tendency is, they would take it cheaply and they would take God cheaply, and they would take the whole process of self-realization cheaply. Therefore, first we must understand the greatness of God. How God is completely transcendental, to any of our limited conceptions of what is reality. First we must establish that there is a God. And that he is the Absolute Truth and the greatness and glory of how he is creating, maintaining and destroying everything that exists. First, we must develop a fear of God. And … and fathomless respect for God. And in this condition we humble ourselves before the Lord, understanding our insignificant position in his almighty glory, and we become his servant.

Lord’s intimate pastimes can be understood by following in the footsteps of the mahajans

And when we capture this and understand such great souls as Dhruva maharaja and the type of tapasya that they performed, understand what the great souls have gone through, to attain God's mercy. What did Dhruva go through to attain God's mercy; such tapasya and austerity and fearless determination. What Prahlada maharaja was willing to endure to attain the mercy of the Lord? How he never gave up his faith and was willing to accept all persecution, all torture, imminent death, due to his great devotion and loyalty to the Supreme Personality of Godhead. How Ambarish was so humble and so gentle, and so forgiving even to someone who was envious of him, trying to kill him. We can see, we can understand the basic qualities of a devotee’s life, through the teachings of Bhagavatam, and when we have a careful study of Bhagavatam in this way, how this material nature is working? Why the spirit soul comes to this material nature? How the spirit soul can free himself by following in the footsteps of these great souls? ‘Mahājano yena gataḥ sa panthāu’: [Cc. Madhya 17.186], then alas, when we come to the tenth canto, and we read about the intimate lila of Krishna with the brajvasis ,then it is no longer just a form of entertainment, which is very sweet and very pleasing to hear. But we can truly appreciate, with devotion, the glories of the pure devotees of Vrindavan and how the Lord is intimately reciprocating with them. And in this mood, we can literally enter into the lila of the Lord, simply through the process of hearing.

Srimad-Bhagavatam should be read attentively; from the very beginning, as it is compared to the body of the Lord

Therefore, knowing that, this is a very, very careful study that must be undertaken. Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati Thankur wanted to stress to all persons, for all time to come, how to properly understand bhagavata philosophy. Therefore, for thirty days, he lectured twice a day, for hours and hours each lecture and he never went past the first sloka. And in the audience somebody, often times people asked ‘when are you're going to go to the next sloka?’ He said this first, he said ‘we can discuss the first word of the first sloka for the rest of eternity, and still not complete the explanation.’ This is the glory of Bhagavatam. Indeed, it is said that every syllable of Bhagavatam, Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati explained, is so pure that by understanding any syllable from any of the cantos, of any of the chapters of Bhagvatam, you will attain self-realization; love of God. Because, the Bhagavatam is non-different than the body of Krishna. Just like the proper devotee, when he approaches Krishna, he never approaches the head of Krishna, he always approaches first the feet. This is proper respect. It is described, that when one comes before the deity, one should always, if one is a respectful vaishnava, first meditate upon the lotus feet of Sri Sri Radhagopinath, and then gradually, gradually go to his calves, and his knees, and his thighs, and his waist, and his chest, his arms, his flute. And then with gradual respectful obeisance, he finally beholds the beautiful moon-like face of Sri Sri Radhagopinath.

Great personalities, who are the representatives of Krishna, ‘vande guroh sri-carnaravindam’[Sri Sri Gurv-astaka], we don't say ‘I worship the lotus face of my spiritual master.’ We say ‘I worship the lotus feet of my spiritual master’. Out of respect, as the servant of guru, we approach him by worshipping his feet; by meditating upon his feet. So, similarly, Srimad-Bhagavatam is non-different than Krishna. It is the literary incarnation of Krishna. Bhagavatam is non-different than the body of Krishna, therefore, a respectful devotee must approach the Bhagavatam as he would approach God himself. The first canto and the second canto are considered to be the feet of the Srimad-Bhagavatam. In fact, according to the great acharyas, one should not dare to go beyond the first two cantos until one is free from sex- life.(laughs)

One should meditate on those lotus feet of the lord and gradually, as one understands the subject matter and is purified and has realized the subject matter, then one goes to the next stage. Otherwise, you simply will not understand. It is just like if you go to school and you go to the first standard but you do not understand, assimilate and realize the subject-matter of the first standard, then you will go to the second standard, you will not, you will be there, you will be superficially going to the lectures, reading the books but you will not understand what it all means. Because the preliminary information which is essential, has not been understood. Therefore, you have to pass your exams, which prove that you have realized the previous subject matter, then you can go on to the next grade.

Though Srimad-Bhagavatam should be read from the first canto, Srila Prabhupada guides us to the lotus feet of the Lord no matter where we start

So one may ask… I remember Srila Prabhupada, he had translated nine cantos of Bhagavatam and devotees were taking any one of the nine cantos, and they were going to the airports distributing them. So, we asked Srila Prabhupada this question that ‘if one must systematically understand Bhagavatam by approaching the feet first and one should not, out of disrespect, try to jump above his position. Then why in the airports, to meat-eating karmis, who are addicted to all sinful activities, are we selling them the seventh canto, the eighth canto, the ninth canto. We're selling them any canto, whichever one they like picture best in the front, we sell them’. Prabhupada explained that, ‘In the Bhagavatam, it is factually offensive, to jump beyond ones realization to the higher cantos, but my purports describe everything according to the elementary fundamental philosophy of Bhagavad-Gita. So, therefore, all the nine cantos, all the ten cantos, all the twelve cantos of Bhagavatam, as I am going to translate them, they all contain the lotus feet of Krishna.’ Even in the description of the face of the Lord, the tenth canto, when you read the purport, Prabhupada immediately brings you back to his feet. And he explains everything according to the fundamental principles of Krishna consciousness. Therefore, you could read any of my books from any place and you will find the lotus feet of Krishna. You will find the basic elementary philosophy of Bhagavatam.

Just like Bhakti Siddhanta Sarasvati Thakur, for one month, he simply explained the first verse of Bhagavatam. So similarly, Srila Prabhupada, he is so expert in presenting Krishna-consciousness to the world, that he has presented practically all the eighteen thousand slokas of Bhagavatam. And by reading all of them with his purports, really, you never leave the first verse. The first verse is there, the reminder of the teachings of the first verse is there, throughout Bhagavatam. So that it is impossible to get a misconception of what is being spoken.

Studying and worshipping Bhagavatam under the guidance of a bona-fide spiritual master culminates in self-realization

And at the same time for those who are elevated in their realization they can… the essence of the highest realizations and the highest knowledge is also, perfectly in full being related in Prabhupada’s books. This is the miracle of the divine touch of the great empowered acharyas. That he has taken the spotless purana, Srimad-Bhagavatam and wherever you read it you get the highest, most, fullest realizations. If you are on that level to understand, and if you are not, you get the basic elementary explanation at each verse. So those who are neophyte devotees could take Krishna book, which is tenth canto, and learn the whole science of devotional service from that. And those who are paramahamsas, on the highest stages, they will relish the ultimate satisfaction of the highest lila of the lord. So, we must accept and admit our own neophyte position; not try to jump over the head of the great spiritual master and approach the Bhagavatam recitation on a level that is beyond our realization, or even approach the scripture itself without the divine protective guidance of the spiritual master. And it is guaranteed. Srila Prabhupada, on behalf of all the greater acharyas, has guaranteed that anyone who makes a careful systematic study of Srimad-Bhagavatam, under the direction of a bona fide spiritual master, by the time he completes this great work he will have attained self-realization, and pure love of God. That is the glory of this great literature.

Therefore, oftentimes, Srimad-Bhagavatam is worshiped. Worshiped with puja, just as God is worshiped with puja. When we begin the class, ‘Om namo bhagavate vasudevaya’[Dwadasaksara mantra], know that we are offering our prostrated obeisances to this great literature, written by Vyasadeva. This great literature, which is absolutely non-different than Vasudeva. He is fully manifesting himself through the divine vibration of bhagavad philosophy. Therefore in these classes of Srimad-Bhagavatam, every morning and in our readings of Srimad-Bhagavatam, we should very seriously take advantage of the most important incarnation in this age.

Simply by chanting and studying Srimad-Bhagavatam and distributing it to others, one can attain perfection

Sri Chaintanya Mahaprabhu described that:

‘yāre dekha, tāre kaha ‘kṛṣṇa’-upadeśa
āmāra ājṣāya guru haṣā tāra’ ei deśa’ [Cc Madhya 7.128]

All that is required in this age of Kali-yuga, is to chant the holy names and to study Srimad-Bhagavatam, under the instruction of a bona-fide guru. That is all that is required in this age. And he orders everyone to become a guru. And how to become a guru? By always chanting the holy names and simply repeating the words of Srimad-Bhagavatam. Anyone, in any position of life, according to Mahaprabhu Sri Chaitanya, can qualify himself to be a guru, if he simply learns the science of bhagavad philosophy and chants the holy names. So, let us take advantage of daily bathing our mind and intellect in the pure vibration emanating from this most exalted of all literatures. And with great humility and devotion, let us praise the glories of Srimad-Bhagavatam to every town, to every village, throughout the world. And then Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's mission, his desire, will be fulfilled. Srila Prabhupada spent the most intimate moments of his life, translating this great literature. And he wanted this literature; his life's desire was that this literature be distributed profusely for the benefit of all living beings. ‘kṛṣṇe sva-dhāmopagate dharma-jṣān ādibhiḥ saha[SB 1.3.43].He wanted this literature, to be distributed in such a way that it would give light to all corners of this world, in this dark age of Kali-yuga. Of course book bhagavatam and person bhagvatam are non-different. So, as well as distributing this great literature, by performing acts of devotion based on the principle of this great scripture, the same effect is achieved. So therefore, it is the responsibility of every devotee, to imbibe within his heart the words of the Bhagavatam. And by acting in surrender and devotion, whatever service we render, we can assist the Lord in giving light to this age of Kali, and thus, the ultimate benediction of Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, we can carry wherever we go and distribute it.

The final verse of Bhagavatam, nama-sankirtanam , describes that, in this age of Kali simply by chanting the holy name of Krishna, all sins are destroyed. And the ultimate benediction of pure love is achieved. Vyasadeva ends Bhagavatam with this sloka. Therefore, on behalf of the great acharyas, we should distribute the essence of bhagvat philosophy by lecturing, by distributing books, by distributing prasad and most of all, by inducing people to hear and chant the holy names of the Lord. And through this process we can please Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, the Yuga- avatar, to the highest degree and that is the perfection of our life. In giving we receive. To the degree we give the essence of the Bhagavatam, on behalf of our guru maharaja to others, to that degree we will understand and realize the essence of the Bhagavatam.

Thank you very much. ‘Hare Krishna’. ‘Is there any questions?’ [Applause]

Q&A Transcription

[Devotee:](not clear/audible) (42.18 - 43.11)

[Maharaja:] ‘You know, in the temples, many, many temples around the world, where they really stressed book distribution; they have a little altar, on the altar, for the Bhagavatam. And along with the deities they do puja to Bhagavatam . So that devotees can imbibe within their hearts the sanctity and the sacredness of this book. And then when they go out and distribute it, they feel like they are holding God himself in their hands and delivering him to the condition [not clear](43.42 - 43.59)

So this..so you see, just as we approach the deity with great respect and reverence, when we come to the class of Srimad-Bhagavatam, we should understand that if the speaker, is a bona-fide speaker, who is explaining bhagavata according to the previous acharyas, then the class itself is non- different than Krishna. The class itself, that vibration is the Srimad-Bhagavatam manifesting. So, with great respect and reverence, we should stay awake and hear the class. And we should be thirsty to receive the mercy of Bhagavatam at every moment during the bhagavat class. And when we pick up the book …And we should be thirsty to receive the mercy of Bhagavatam..And when we pick up the book, Srimad Bhagvatam to read it, we must understand that, it is Krishna himself directly speaking to me, through the lips of Srila Prabhupada and the great acharyas. Krishna reveals himself as you approach him. If you take him for granted, if you take him cheaply, he will reveal himself in the same way to you. But if you approach the bhagavad class, the book bhagavat and the person bhagvat, with sincere respect, with a humble and devoted heart, then you will receive the highest knowledge of devotion, by the mercy of the book bhagavat and the person bhagavat;

Vāsudeve bhagavati
bhakti-yogaḥ prayojitaḥ
janayaty āśu vairāgyaṁ
jṣānaṁ ca yad ahaitukam 

[SB 4.29.37]

[By rendering devotional service unto the Personality of Godhead, Sri Krsna, one immediately acquires causeless knowledge and detachment from the world.]

Bhagavatam is teaching bhakti. After achieving one attains all the benefits of jnan and vairagya, they are included. This is the great gift, of those who embody the teachings of Srimad-Bhagavatam. They are delivering pure bhakti to the world. And the price is faith and sincere aural reception.

[Maharaja:]‘ Are there any other questions ?’

[Devotee:] (Not Clear/audible)(47.17- 47.48)

[Maharaja:] ‘Srila Prabhu…Srila Prabhupada explained to us, that these books are so sacred, that if someone even touches the book, they become so purified that they will take a human life in their next birth. He described that, if somebody takes this book and just puts it on his bookshelf, and never even reads it, just the presence of that book within his heart, within his home, will radiate such pure spiritual energy that his whole..on a subtle level, his whole household will become purified, just from the invisible spiritual emanations that are coming from that book. And it is guaranteed that that person will become a devotee.

Sometimes, yes.

INAUDIBLE Interception(49.01-49.04)

As far as, possible we should educate them. I remember, in New York City in 1972, I was present when Srila Prabhupada was giving a class. And at that time the whole temple was organized, in such a way that the emphasis was book distribution, book distribution, book distribution. So there were hundreds and hundreds of devotees crowded in the temple. So crowded was the temple and practically all of them were dedicated to book distribution. And Prabhupada was chastising the devotees, he said, “You go out all day and you distribute these books, but my question is, do you read them and do you understand them? If you do not read them yourself and if you do not understand them yourself very carefully, how will you convince anybody else to take these books seriously” If somebody asks you a very careful question about the subject matter in the book and you say ‘well I do not know because I don't have time to read them, I only sell them.’ That person will think, well, ‘you're trying to convince me to read it but you do not read it.’ You will not convince anyone. Therefore, one must personally study and understand, under the direction of guru, the teachings of Srimad-Bhagavatam, and with that conviction we can convince others. We can teach them how to properly study Bhagavatam and attain pure devotional service.

Of course when distributing books on the streets, we may not have time to give them a full explanation of the science of devotional service. But, you may only have the time to convince them, that this is a very, very important spiritual book, which will make your life better. That may be all you have time to explain to someone. But your conviction and the purity of your purpose, will very much affect that person's heart. And that, even more than what you say, will influence his life.

[Devotee:] (not audible)(51.48 - 52.27)

[Maharaja:] Prabhupada described that these books are like time- bombs. Even if they go into the hands of someone who has no interest in them, in due course of time it will explode. I can tell you stories which will make your head spin, personal experience of the power of these books. There are dozens and dozens and dozens and dozens and dozens of devotees, who have surrendered their lives to Krishna, by third hand, getting a book that was thrown in the garbage. As soon as the book is put into the circulation of this world, whoever takes it, you do not know where it will end up and what the result will be. So, therefore, there is only positive gain when these books go out into the world, to circulate within this material creation. He just found a book in an airplane. Someone was at an airport, he got the book, like this is ‘useless Hare Krishna book’, put it in the airplane; just left it on the floor. Next devotee got on the plane, the next person didn't know anything about spiritual life, picks up the book; it changed his life. He got off the airplane and he was just thinking. He was… when he got off the airplane, he was chanting, “Hare Krsna Hare Krsna Krsna Krsna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Hare Hare.” And then in the airport that he got off at, there were devotees there selling books. So, some people you know they, they don't look like devotees, when they sell books. They wear suits, ties. So this guy got off the plane, he was going on “Hare Krsna Hare Krsna Krsna Krsna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare.” Oh! the devotees got on this; Ah! this is a good person to sell a book to. “Sir”, stopped him. ‘Oh! You’re the ones that sell these books?’ He had the book in his hand. He says I was just reading this book, it's just changed my life. I want to become a devotee. Do you know where I can find these people? He thought this person was just a bookseller. He said, ‘I am a devotee.’ He said ‘I've never met a devotee, now I want to be a devotee’. So they took him to the temple and he became a devotee that day. So God knows, when you distribute a book whose hands is going to end up in and what.. how it's going to influence his life. Krishna is in everyone's heart. These books, Prabhupada says are like time-bombs, in due course of time each book will go off.(laughs)

But of course we should, by all means, try to distribute books with this higher quality of understanding when we give it to another as possible. That is the best. (Not Clear)

[Devotee:]( not audible)(56.00 - 56.26)

[Maharaja:] ‘There is certainly..they remind people of Krishna. But this book you have to understand, it's not just the Bhagavatam, but it's the mercy of the pure devotee and all the previous acharyas, that is fully manifesting itself in this book. There is immense value.

You have to understand that this book, Prabhupada’s life and soul and the life and soul of all the previous acharyas, who Parbhupada was being inspired by, is fully manifested in this book. It's not just a karmi that makes a little statue. Certainly that statue can remind people of Krishna, that's very nice. But this book is the deity of Krishna, which is being revealed through the most intimate mercy of the acharyas.

Thank you very much

© Radhanath Swami Lecture Transcripts.
